49	\(\chi\)-identity proof of REF

184	  supported on a finite set \(\{Q : n_Q \neq 0\}\)
185	  (REF). The \emph{invertible sheaf
186	  \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) associated to \(D\)}, also written
187	  \(\mathcal O_C(D) := \mathscr L(D)\) in Hartshorne's notation, is the
188	  sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of the constant sheaf
189	  \(\mathcal K_C \cong K(C)\) (Proposition~6.15: on an integral scheme the
190	  sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
191	  field) defined section-wise by
192	  \[
193	    \Gamma\bigl(U, \mathcal O_C(D)\bigr) \;:=\;
194	    \{\, 0 \,\} \;\cup\;
195	    \bigl\{\, f \in K(C)^{\times} \;\big|\;
196	      \ord_Q(f) \;\geq\; -\,n_Q \text{ for every prime divisor }
197	      Q \text{ of } C \text{ with } Q \in U \bigr\}
198	  \]
199	  for each open \(U \subseteq C\). Restriction along \(V \hookrightarrow U\)
200	  is the identity on the underlying carrier \(K(C)\), which preserves
201	  membership (a smaller open imposes \emph{fewer} order constraints, so
202	  the constraint set on \(U\) embeds into the constraint set on \(V\)). The
203	  \(\bar k\)-module structure on
204	  \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D))\) is restricted from the
205	  \(\bar k\)-module structure on \(K(C)\) (a \(\bar k\)-scalar multiple of a
206	  rational function has the same order at every prime divisor).
207	
208	  \paragraph{Equivalence with the Cartier-divisor description.} The
209	  formal sum \(D = \sum_Q n_Q [Q]\) packages, on a curve where prime
210	  divisors are exactly closed points and the local rings at prime
211	  divisors are DVRs (smoothness \(+\) dimension~\(1\)), the Cartier-divisor
212	  data \(\{(U_i, f_i)\}\) of Hartshorne's definition: locally on a small
213	  open \(U_i\) containing exactly one prime divisor \(Q\) in the support of
214	  \(D\), pick a uniformiser \(\pi_Q \in K(C)\) at \(Q\) and set
215	  \(f_i := \pi_Q^{n_Q}\); on opens disjoint from the support of \(D\), set
216	  \(f_i := 1\). The transitions on overlaps \(U_i \cap U_j\) are units in
217	  \(\mathcal O_{C, Q}^{\times}\) for each prime \(Q\) in the overlap (any
218	  two uniformisers differ by a unit, by the DVR structure), so the
219	  \(\{(U_i, f_i)\}\) define a Cartier divisor whose Weil-divisor image
220	  under Hartshorne's (6.11) is the original \(D\). The
221	  ``\(f_i^{-1}\)-generated'' subsheaf of \(\mathcal K_C\) from Hartshorne's
222	  definition then coincides with the section-wise description above:
223	  the local generator \(f_i^{-1} = \pi_Q^{-n_Q}\) has order \(-n_Q\) at \(Q\),
224	  so the local sections are exactly \(f \in K(C)\) with
225	  \(\ord_Q(f \cdot \pi_Q^{n_Q}) \geq 0\), i.e.\ \(\ord_Q(f) \geq -n_Q\), on
226	  \(U_i\).
227	
228	  Hartshorne's Proposition~6.13(a) (quoted in
229	  REF of REF)
230	  then guarantees that the resulting subsheaf $\mathcal O_C(D) \subset
231	  \mathcal K_C$ is an invertible sheaf on $C$, independently of the
232	  choice of uniformisers \(\pi_Q\) (any two choices differ by a unit, so
233	  they generate the same \(\mathcal O_C\)-submodule).
234	
235	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
236	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf} takes a Weil
237	  divisor \(D : C.\text{left.WeilDivisor}\) and returns a
238	  \(\texttt{Sheaf}~J~(\mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\) on
239	  \(C.\text{left.toTopCat}\), where
240	  \(J = \texttt{Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat}\) is the
241	  standard topology on the topological space underlying \(C\). The
242	  closed-point specialisation \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) is
243	  REF of
244	  REF; their agreement at \(D = [P]\) is
245	  REF below. The construction follows
246	  the iter-182 \texttt{ocofp-sheaf-internalhom} recipe in
247	  \texttt{analogies/}: presheaf-by-hand via per-open
248	  \(\bar k\)-submodules of the function field, sheaf property reduced to
249	  the locality of the order conditions at the stalks of the prime
250	  divisors. The declaration is \texttt{noncomputable}.
251	\end{definition}

255	\begin{lemma}\leanok
256	[Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf]
257	  \label{lem:sheafOf_zero}
258	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero}
259	  \uses{def:sheafOf}
260	
261	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
262	  \(\bar k\). Then the invertible sheaf associated to the zero divisor
263	  \(0 \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) is canonically the structure sheaf:
264	  \[
265	    \mathcal O_C(0) \;=\; \mathcal O_C
266	  \]
267	  as sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-modules of \(\mathcal K_C\), equivalently as
268	  objects in \(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\).
269	\end{lemma}
270	
271	\begin{proof}
272	  
273	  At \(D = 0\) the coefficient \(n_Q = 0\) at every prime divisor \(Q\), so the
274	  section condition of REF reduces, on every open
275	  \(U \subseteq C\), to
276	  \[
277	    \Gamma\bigl(U, \mathcal O_C(0)\bigr)
278	    \;=\; \{ 0 \} \cup \{ f \in K(C)^{\times} \;|\;
279	      \ord_Q(f) \geq 0 \text{ for every prime divisor } Q \in U \}.
280	  \]
281	  By the local description of the structure sheaf on the integral
282	  scheme \(C\) (the regular functions on \(U\) are exactly the rational
283	  functions whose order is non-negative at every prime divisor of
284	  \(C\) landing in \(U\); this is the standard ``non-negative order \(=\)
285	  regular'' identification, e.g.\ Hartshorne~II~\S6 immediately before
286	  Proposition~6.11), the right-hand side is precisely
287	  \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C)\). The restriction maps on both sides are
288	  the identity on \(K(C)\), so the equality of presheaves promotes to an
289	  equality of sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-modules of \(\mathcal K_C\).
290	\end{proof}

293	[Sheaf at a single closed point is the line bundle of
294	REF]
295	  \label{lem:sheafOf_singlePoint}
296	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_singlePoint}

298	        def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint}

300	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
301	  \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point, viewed as a Weil divisor
302	  \([P] \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) via REF. Then the
303	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) of REF agrees on
304	  the nose with the line bundle of the closed point
305	  \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) of REF:
306	  \[
307	    \mathcal O_C(D)\bigl|_{D = [P]}
308	    \;=\; \texttt{lineBundleAtClosedPoint}_{P}
309	  \]

312	\end{lemma}

314	\begin{proof}
315	  
316	  Unfold both definitions. On the \texttt{sheafOf} side, the formal sum
317	  \([P]\) has coefficients \(n_Q = 1\) if \(Q = P\) and \(n_Q = 0\) otherwise
318	  (where \(P\) is identified with the prime divisor it determines by
319	  REF). Thus
320	  \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) contains \(0\) and those
321	  \(f \in K(C)^{\times}\) satisfying \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\) for every prime
322	  divisor \(Q \in U\) with \(Q \neq P\), plus \(\ord_P(f) \geq -1\) if
323	  \(P \in U\).
324	
325	  On the REF side, the same conditions
326	  are exactly the ones extracted by
327	  REF of
328	  \texttt{RR.3}, at every open \(U\) (not only at the global section
329	  \(U = C\)): a rational function \(f \in K(C)\) is a section of
330	  \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) on \(U\) iff it lies in the structure sheaf at every
331	  \(Q \in U \setminus \{P\}\) and has at most a simple pole at \(P\) when

335	  sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-modules of \(\mathcal K_C\) are therefore equal

343	\begin{lemma}\leanok
344	[Short exact sequence for \(D \rightsquigarrow D + [Y]\)]
345	  \label{lem:sheafOf_ses_single_add}
346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add}
347	  \uses{def:sheafOf, def:divisor_closed_point, def:prime_divisor}
348	
349	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, p.~296 (the inductive step of the proof
350	  % of Theorem~1.3, ``Next, let \(D\) be any divisor, and let \(P\) be any
351	  % point...''). Read from
352	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 313,
353	  % transcribed character-by-character from the rendered scanned page
354	  % image (the PDF has no text layer).
355	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We consider \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(X\). Its
356	  % structure sheaf is a skyscraper sheaf \(k\) sitting at the point \(P\),
357	  % which we denote by \(k(P)\), and its ideal sheaf is \(\mathscr L(-P)\)
358	  % by (II, 6.18). Therefore we have an exact sequence
359	  % \(0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0\).
360	  % Tensoring with \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) we get
361	  % \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\).
362	  % (Since \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by
363	  % it does not affect the sheaf \(k(P)\).)"
364	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~296, the inductive step of the
365	  proof of Theorem~1.3.}
366	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
367	  \(\bar k\), let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) be a Weil divisor, and let \(P \in C\) be
368	  a closed point (equivalently, a prime divisor
369	  REF on a smooth curve). Then the inclusion
370	  \(\mathcal O_C(D) \hookrightarrow \mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) of
371	  sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-modules of \(\mathcal K_C\) fits into a short exact
372	  sequence
373	  \[
374	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(D)
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; k(P)
377	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
378	  \]
379	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\) (equivalently, in
380	  \(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\)), where \(k(P)\) denotes the
381	  skyscraper sheaf at \(P\) with stalk \(\bar k\) (the residue field of \(P\)
382	  is \(\bar k\) since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(P\) is a
383	  \(\bar k\)-rational closed point).
384	\end{lemma}
385	
386	\begin{proof}
387	  
388	  Hartshorne's argument (IV.1.3, the inductive step quoted above)
389	  proceeds in two beats:
390	
391	  \emph{Beat 1 (the ideal-sheaf SES at \(P\)).} The closed-subscheme
392	  inclusion \(P \hookrightarrow C\) has structure sheaf
393	  \(\mathcal O_P \cong k(P)\) (a skyscraper at \(P\) with stalk \(\bar k\)),
394	  and Hartshorne's Proposition~II.6.18 identifies its ideal sheaf as
395	  \(\mathcal I_P = \mathcal O_C(-[P])\) (the sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of
396	  \(\mathcal K_C\) generated by a local uniformiser \(\pi_P\) at \(P\) and by
397	  \(1\) away from \(P\), i.e.\ the locally-principal ideal of the locally
398	  principal closed subscheme \(P\)). This produces the structural short
399	  exact sequence
400	  \[
401	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(-[P])
402	    \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C
403	    \;\longrightarrow\; k(P)
404	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
405	  \]
406	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\).
407	
408	  \emph{Beat 2 (tensor with \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\)).} The line bundle
409	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) is locally free of rank~\(1\)
410	  (Hartshorne~II.6.13(a), REF), so $- \otimes_{\mathcal O_C}
411	  \mathcal O_C(D + [P])$ is exact on $\mathbf{Coh}(C)$ and preserves
412	  the short exact sequence of Beat~1:
413	  \[
414	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \otimes
415	      \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
416	    \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C \otimes \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
417	    \;\longrightarrow\; k(P) \otimes \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
418	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
419	  \]
420	  Three identifications collapse this to the claimed sequence:
421	  \begin{itemize}
422	    \item $\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \otimes \mathcal O_C(D + [P]) \cong
423	      \mathcal O_C(D)$ (tensor product of line bundles is the
424	      \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\)-additive operation $D' + D'' \mapsto \mathcal O_C(D' +
425	      D'')$ via Hartshorne's Proposition~6.13(b), which is in turn the
426	      ring-theoretic identity
427	      $\pi_P^{-1} \cdot \pi_P^{1 + n_P^{D}} \cdot \prod_{Q \neq P}
428	      \pi_Q^{n_Q^{D}} = \prod_Q \pi_Q^{n_Q^{D}}$ on the generating
429	      rational functions, where \(n_Q^{D}\) is the coefficient of \(Q\)
430	      in \(D\));
431	    \item $\mathcal O_C \otimes \mathcal O_C(D + [P]) \cong
432	      \mathcal O_C(D + [P])$ (the structure sheaf is the tensor unit);
433	    \item \(k(P) \otimes \mathcal O_C(D + [P]) \cong k(P)\) (tensoring a
434	      skyscraper at \(P\) by a locally free rank-\(1\) sheaf preserves the
435	      skyscraper; concretely, at the stalk over \(P\) the line bundle
436	      \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])_P\) is a free \(\mathcal O_{C,P}\)-module of
437	      rank one, and $k(P) \otimes_{\mathcal O_{C,P}} \mathcal O_{C,P}
438	      \cong k(P)$ is just $k(P)$; away from $P$ the skyscraper is the
439	      zero sheaf, on which the tensor is the zero sheaf).
440	  \end{itemize}
441	  Substituting yields
442	  \[
443	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(D)
444	    \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
445	    \;\longrightarrow\; k(P)
446	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
447	  \]
448	  exact in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\), as required.
449	
450	  \emph{Compatibility with the structural inclusion.} The first map
451	  \(\mathcal O_C(D) \hookrightarrow \mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) is the
452	  inclusion of subsheaves of \(\mathcal K_C\): at the level of sections,
453	  the constraint \(\ord_P(f) \geq -n_P^{D}\) of
454	  \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D))\) implies the weaker constraint
455	  \(\ord_P(f) \geq -n_P^{D} - 1\) of \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D + [P]))\)
456	  when \(P \in U\) (constraints on \(Q \neq P\) are unchanged), and the map
457	  is identity on \(K(C)\) at the carrier level. The second map
458	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P]) \twoheadrightarrow k(P)\) is the evaluation
459	  ``leading coefficient at the pole at \(P\)'' (i.e.\ the
460	  \(\pi_P^{-(n_P^{D} + 1)}\)-coefficient in the Laurent expansion of \(f\)
461	  at \(P\)), which is well-defined on \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D + [P]))\)
462	  modulo the subgroup of sections with \(\ord_P(f) \geq -n_P^{D}\) ---
463	  i.e.\ modulo \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D))\).
464	\end{proof}

466	\section{Sheaf-property correctness}
467	
468	The construction of REF is presheaf-by-hand: a
469	per-open \(\bar k\)-submodule of \(K(C)\), with restriction along
470	\(V \hookrightarrow U\) given by the identity on \(K(C)\). This section
471	sketches the verifications that make the construction land in the
472	target category \(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\).
473	
474	\paragraph{Functoriality of the carrier.} For \(V \subseteq U\), the
475	constraint set defining \(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D))\) is a \emph{subset}
476	of the constraint set defining \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal O_C(D))\) (fewer
477	prime divisors to constrain at), so the identity on \(K(C)\) restricts
478	to a well-defined inclusion
479	\(\Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D)) \hookrightarrow \Gamma(V, \mathcal O_C(D))\).
480	Composition \(W \subseteq V \subseteq U\) is again identity-on-\(K(C)\), so
481	strict functoriality holds.
482	
483	\paragraph{\(\bar k\)-linearity.} A \(\bar k\)-scalar \(\lambda \in \bar k\)
484	acts on \(K(C)\) by multiplication, and
485	\(\ord_Q(\lambda \cdot f) = \ord_Q(f)\) for \(\lambda \neq 0\)
486	(units have order zero); for \(\lambda = 0\), \(\lambda \cdot f = 0\) is
487	included by convention. Thus the constraint set is a
488	\(\bar k\)-submodule and the restriction maps are \(\bar k\)-linear.
489	
490	\paragraph{Sheaf property.} The order condition
491	``\(\ord_Q(f) \geq -n_Q\) for every prime divisor \(Q \in U\)'' is
492	\emph{stalk-local} at each \(Q\): it depends only on the germ of \(f\) at
493	the point of \(C\) underlying \(Q\), equivalently on the image of \(f\) in
494	the DVR \(\mathcal O_{C, Q}\). Consequently the sheaf property reduces
495	to two ingredients:
496	\begin{enumerate}
497	  \item \emph{The presheaf-of-sets underlying \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) is a
498	    subpresheaf of the constant presheaf at \(K(C)\).} The constant
499	    presheaf at \(K(C)\) is a sheaf (every locally-constant section of a
500	    constant presheaf on a topological space is globally constant when
501	    the space is irreducible, which the topological space underlying an
502	    integral scheme is). A subpresheaf of a sheaf is itself a sheaf iff
503	    its membership condition is local; the order conditions are local
504	    at each prime divisor's underlying point, so the membership
505	    condition is local across an open cover and a section glued from
506	    locally-defined sections inherits the order condition at every
507	    prime divisor.
508	  \item \emph{The forget-functor bridge from
509	    \(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\) to
510	    \(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Set})\).} The categorical forget
511	    \(\mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k} \to \mathbf{Set}\) creates limits, hence
512	    reflects the sheaf property: a presheaf of \(\bar k\)-modules is a
513	    sheaf iff its underlying presheaf of sets is. Combined with~(i),
514	    this delivers the sheaf property for the
515	    \(\bar k\)-module-valued \(\mathcal O_C(D)\).
516	\end{enumerate}
517	
518	\paragraph{Invertibility.} On any open \(U \subseteq C\) disjoint from
519	the support of \(D\), the constraint set is exactly the structure-sheaf
520	constraint \(\ord_Q(f) \geq 0\) for every prime divisor \(Q \in U\); hence
521	\(\mathcal O_C(D)|_U = \mathcal O_C|_U\), and the trivialisation
522	\(\mathcal O_C(D)|_U \cong \mathcal O_C|_U\) is the identity. On a small
523	open \(U \subseteq C\) containing exactly one prime divisor \(Q\) from the
524	support of \(D\), the local generator \(\pi_Q^{-n_Q} \in K(C)\) trivialises
525	\(\mathcal O_C(D)|_U\) as a free rank-\(1\) \(\mathcal O_C|_U\)-module: a
526	section \(f \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C(D))\) satisfies $\ord_Q(f) \geq
527	-n_Q$ iff $\pi_Q^{n_Q} \cdot f \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal O_C)$, and the
528	``multiplication by \(\pi_Q^{n_Q}\)'' map is an
529	\(\mathcal O_C|_U\)-linear isomorphism
530	\(\mathcal O_C(D)|_U \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal O_C|_U\). The two types
531	of trivialising opens cover \(C\) (any prime divisor lies in at most
532	finitely many supports across a covering, and the support of \(D\) is
533	finite); patching the trivialisations on overlaps amounts to changing
534	uniformiser, which is unit-times the original (by the DVR property), so
535	the cocycle is in \(\mathcal O_C^{\times}\) and the sheaf is invertible.
536	
537	The closure of (i)+(ii) in Lean follows the project's existing
538	\texttt{Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf} /
539	\texttt{toModuleKPresheaf\_isSheaf} template in
540	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/}, which
541	landed the analogous bottom-up
542	\(\mathbf{Sh}(C, \mathbf{Mod}_{\bar k})\)-valued construction for the
543	structure sheaf. The order-condition locality is closed via the project's
544	\texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order} stalk bridge of
545	\texttt{RR.1} (REF and surrounding lemmas).
